package repository

import (
	"fmt"
	"net/http"

	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Repository returns errors that embed HTTP status codes.
// Repositories should return domain-level errors, not HTTP transport errors.

type Inventory struct {
	ID       string `gorm:"column:id"`
	Product  string `gorm:"column:product"`
	Quantity int    `gorm:"column:quantity"`
}

type InventoryRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

// FindByID returns an error with HTTP status code embedded - anti-pattern.
func (r *InventoryRepository) FindByID(id string) (*Inventory, error) {
	var item Inventory
	if err := r.db.First(&item, "id = ?", id).Error; err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("http.StatusNotFound: %w", err)
	}
	return &item, nil
}

// Create returns HTTP status error - anti-pattern.
func (r *InventoryRepository) Create(item *Inventory) error {
	if err := r.db.Create(item).Error; err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("http.StatusConflict: %w", err)
	}
	return nil
}

// Delete returns HTTP status error - anti-pattern.
func (r *InventoryRepository) Delete(id string) error {
	result := r.db.Delete(&Inventory{}, "id = ?", id)
	if result.Error != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("http.StatusInternalServerError: %w", result.Error)
	}
	if result.RowsAffected == 0 {
		return fmt.Errorf("http.StatusNotFound: item %s not found", id)
	}
	return nil
}
